causal_chain.from: Increased kinetic energy of water molecules (due to heating)
causal_chain.mechanism: Heating water increases the average speed of its molecules. Faster molecules have more energy to overcome intermolecular forces and transition into the gaseous phase.
Boiling occurs when the vapor pressure of the water equals the surrounding atmospheric pressure.
The boiling point is the temperature at which vapor pressure equals atmospheric pressure. At sea level, atmospheric pressure is standard, thus defining the boiling point.

causal_chain.to: Temperature of water remains at 100 degrees Celsius (at sea level)
causal_chain.mechanism: During boiling, the energy input is used to change the state of water from liquid to gas, rather than increasing the temperature. This maintains a constant temperature until all the water has boiled.
